PR surface: Source +180, Tests +202. Total +382 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: unclear. for live reproduction: source inspection shows current main hydrates thinking/model catalog data on reply/model-selection paths, but this review did not run an exact current-main profiler scenario.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid runtime model hydration on hot paths
target.body:
## Summary

This extracts the model-selection hot-path cleanup from #85941 into a focused PR.

- avoid runtime model catalog hydration on reply/model-selection hot paths
- add shared model-ref helpers so callers can carry prepared model facts forward
- update reply model selection and session status surfaces to use the cheaper prepared data
- keep the original contributor commit author: Keshav's Bot <keshavbotagent@gmail.com>

Split from #85941 so model-routing behavior can be reviewed independently from reply delivery and plugin changes. Thanks @keshavbotagent for the original work.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...origin/codex/split-85941-model-hydration`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reply/model-selection paths avoid rediscovering runtime model metadata when the caller already has enough prepared model information.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on fresh `origin/main` split branch.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...origin/codex/split-85941-model-hydration`.
Evidence after fix: split branch cherry-picked cleanly onto current `origin/main`; whitespace/conflict sanity passed.
Observed result after fix: no diff-check errors; commit author preserved as Keshav's Bot <keshavbotagent@gmail.com>.
What was not tested: focused model-selection Vitest was not rerun after the split; CI should run the normal PR gates.
